title: WASM target — timer and async I/O integration
purpose: WASM environments provide timers (setTimeout) and async I/O (fetch) through JavaScript APIs. This brief bridges beamr's timer wheel and NIF calls to browser/worker APIs via wasm-bindgen.

requirements.title: Timer bridge to setTimeout
requirements.spec: Map beamr timer events to browser setTimeout/setInterval.
acceptance: Timer expiry triggers via JavaScript setTimeout callback
Receive timeouts work correctly in WASM
Test: process with receive-after timeout fires correctly in WASM

requirements.title: Async NIF bridge
requirements.spec: NIFs that perform I/O return Promises in WASM; the process suspends until the Promise resolves.
acceptance: NIF returning a Promise suspends the process
Promise resolution wakes the process with the result
Promise rejection maps to a NIF error
Test: fetch URL via async NIF in WASM

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement WebSocket distribution — out of scope
SHALL NOT implement Web Workers threading — single-thread only
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
